_meta.name: Existing booking enquiry — returning guest
_meta.description: Returning guest mentions they already have a booking and asks to check it. Bot should ask for identifying info (booking code / name / phone) or hand off to staff — it must NOT create a new booking, must NOT create a new hold, and must NOT assume the guest's booking details.
_meta.payload_format: Meta-envelope (WhatsApp path). POST the whole file — n8n wraps body under input.body; Normalize reads input.body.entry[0].changes[0].value. Underscored keys are ignored.

_webhook.note: POST this entire file. n8n nests it under input.body. Normalize Incoming Message reads the Meta-envelope via input.body.entry[0].changes[0].value.messages[0].

text.body: Hey, I already booked for April 10 to April 17. Can you check my booking?

_assertions.expected_draft_behavior: Ask for booking reference or name to look up the existing booking. Must NOT create a new booking or hold. Must NOT assume booking details. If booking cannot be verified safely, hand off to staff.
_assertions.expected_handoff: maybe — if bot cannot verify booking safely
_assertions.expected_handoff_reason: Cannot confirm booking without reference; staff should verify

_guardrail_check.note: Per §3x.1 — bot must NOT create a new booking when guest already has one. Per §3x.5 — bot must NOT reveal or guess booking details without verification.
_guardrail_check.pass_condition: Draft asks for booking reference or name (or hands off). No new hold created. No booking_beds write. No payment write.

staff_review.knowledge_gap: Does bot correctly distinguish a status enquiry from a new booking request? Does it ask for a booking reference before attempting any lookup?
